title: liparis loeselii subsp. loeselii
intro: Liparis loeselii subsp. loeselii, also known as the Loesel's twayblade, is a type of terrestrial orchid that belongs to the Orchidaceae family. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its roots, which contain bioactive compounds that have been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Liparis loeselii subsp. loeselii are said to include reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and potentially aiding in the treatment of various diseases such as fever and rheumatism. Herbal preparations of this plant have been made in the form of decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be cautioned as some sources suggest it may cause allergic reactions or interact with certain medications.
